title: forbidden, dense ethiopian rhubarb tibs
description: a comforting and buttery dish featuring forced rhubarb, inspired by the simplicity of shojin cuisine. the rhubarb is pounded with a mortar and pestle to create a unique texture.
instructions: pound the forced rhubarb with a mortar and pestle until it reaches a dense and fibrous texture.
heat the butter in a pan over medium heat.
add the chopped onion and saut9 the until translucent.
add the minced garlic and grated ginger, and saut9 for an additional 1 minute.
add the powdered allspice, black pepper, and ground clove, and stir to combine.
add the pounded rhubarb to the pan and stir to combine.
cook for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the rhubarb is tender.
serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
serve in small portions, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
